title: Simulate Period-End Closing
body_html:
Simulate Period-End Closing

Simulate the period-end closing of treasury positions including valuation, accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues, and classification.

App ID: TPM_SIM_VAL

Use

The simulated period-end closing uses the framework of the operative period-end closing functions. Therefore, the simulated valuation includes, for example, all relevant valuation results that are also generated by the real valuation during period-end closing.

  • The simulated valuation is based on the book value and stores the results without generating persistent flows and postings from the subledger.

    The treasury positions are valued in exactly the same way as for the real key date valuation, in accordance with the settings in the position management procedures.

  • Accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues

    Accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues is used to determine expenses and revenues from financial transactions or positions for a given period and to assign them to the correct accounting period, irrespective of when they were due.

    The simulation of the accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues can be executed for money market transactions and bonds.

  • Classification

    Classification is the last step of the valuation process of the designated subpositions of hedging relationships. It splits the valuation result according to the hedge accounting rule into the different position components, which reflect effective and ineffective amounts of the designated components, the non-designated components, and the amortization.

  • A message log is shown before you can navigate to the results list of the run.

    Each row represents a treasury position and shows the results, such as the new book value in position currency and in valuation currency as well as all other new position component values.

Features

  • During the simulated period-end closing, checks on the real operation runs are not performed. This means, for example, that the business transactions and derived business transactions before the key date don’t have to have the status Fixed for the simulated valuation. In addition, the system doesn’t check the availability of current market data for the key date.

  • If you perform the simulated period-end closing on a date on which you’ve already simulated period-end closing, the results of the last run executed are stored in the database tables and the results of the previous run are deleted from the database table. For a treasury position, for example, only one simulated valuation result is stored in the database tables for a given date.

  • If a treasury position changes before the key date of a simulated period-end closing run that has already been executed, you can recalculate the outdated simulated period-end closing results.

  • You can carry out the simulated valuation, the simulated accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues, and the simulated classification together or individually.
    • If you execute simulated valuation, the simulated accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues, and the simulated classification together, the sequence is as follows:

      1. Simulated valuation

      2. Simulated accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues

      3. Simulated classification

    • If you execute the simulated classification individually, you need to have already carried out the simulated valuation on this day.

  • Simulated period-end closing can be executed in Test Run mode.

  • Simulated period-end closing results can be deleted from the database tables using the Delete Results of Simulated Period-End Closing app.

Prerequisites

  • Make sure that the market data (such as exchange rates and interest rates) are up to date because the simulated period-end closing operations require current market data.

  • NPV values need to be stored in advance of the valuation of treasury positions and of the classification. You can enter the NPVs manually (using the Enter Net Present Values app) or start the calculation of the NPVs using Calculate Net Present Values - Including CVA and DVA. For more information, see also Overview: Calculating NPVs in TRM

  • You must execute the simulated valuation before or together with the simulated classification.

Note

The system doesn’t check the availability of current market data for the key date.

Procedure

  1. Open the app on the SAP Fiori launchpad or call transaction TPM_SIM_VAL in the back-end system.

  2. Select the treasury positions for which you want to perform the simulated valuation. To do this, you must choose the relevant product groups.
    Note

    If you don’t choose a product group, no treasury positions are selected.

    In addition, you can use the fields available under General Selections to restrict the selection of the treasury positions. The General Selections area provides the selection criteria Company Code, Valuation Area, Product Type, and Valuation Class as relevant for all product groups as well as selection criteria relevant for specific product groups.

  3. In the Simulation Parameter area, you control how the report is run:

    • If you want to recalculate outdated simulated valuation results of the selected treasury positions, set the Recalculate Outdated Sim. Val. indicator. The recalculation can be executed for outdated treasury positions for a specific date or period, or for all outdated treasury positions. Enter the date range for recalculation according to your needs.

    • If you want to carry out simulated period-end closing, you must enter the simulation date as the key date.

    • You can select an Exchange Rate Type that is then relevant for the exchange rates that are read from the market data tables during valuation and accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues. If you do not enter an exchange rate type here, the system uses the exchange rate type derived from the relevant position management procedures of the treasury positions.

      Note

      The NPV and hedge accounting key figures (market value components) relevant for valuation and classification are entered manually or calculated using the Calculate Net Present Values - Including CVA and DVA app before you start the simulation. In the Calculate Net Present Values - Including CVA and DVA app, you can select an specific evaluation type for simulation with the required exchange rate types for NPV calculation. In this case, you do not set the Automatic Derivation of Evaluation Parameters indicator in the Calculate Net Present Values - Including CVA and DVA app. Store the calculated NPVs with the Price / NPV Type of valuation procedures of the relevant position management procedures because the simulated period-end closing uses NPVs with these price / NPV types.

      For more information, see also Calculate Net Present Values - Including CVA and DVA.

    • Choose which function you want to execute:

      • Valuation

      • Accrual/Deferral

      • Classification

      You can carry out the simulated valuation, the simulated accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues, and the simulated classification together or individually.
      • If you execute simulated valuation, the simulated accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues, and the simulated classification together, the sequence is as follows:

        1. Simulated valuation

        2. Simulated accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues

        3. Simulated classification

      • If you execute the simulated classification individually, you need to have already carried out the simulated valuation on this day.

    • You can set the Test Run indicator, if needed.

    • For the accrual/deferral of expenses and revenues function, you can in addition set two indicators in the Accrual/Deferral Parameter area:

      • Including Key Date

        Using this indicator, you decide whether or not the key date is within the time period in question.

        The time period is defined for the accrual/deferral function by the key date and the inclusion indicator for the key date.

        Example
        There are two interest flows, Z1 and Z2.

        • Z1 is calculated from 1/1/01 through 12/31/01

        • Z2 is calculated from 1/1/02 through 12/31/02

        If you carry out an accrual/deferral with key date 01/01/02 with the inclusion indicator set to inclusive (technical value 1), Z2 is accrued/deferred for the calculation period "one day".

        If the inclusion indicator is set to exclusive (technical value 0), Z1 is accrued/deferred for the calculation period [1/1/01; 12/31/01].

      • Key Date Is Month End

        If the relevant date is the last day of the month, it needs to be regarded as the month end for corresponding interest calculation methods.

  4. You can choose a layout for the result list.

  5. Choose Execute.

Result

  • For the selected treasury positions, the selected period-end closing operations are executed on the entered simulation date according to the settings of the position management procedure and the relevant configurations for the period-end closing operations based on the current market data.

  • A message log is shown for the simulated period-end closing run.

  • The results are shown in the results list. Each row represents a treasury position and shows the simulation results, such as the new book value in position currency and in valuation currency and also all other new position component values.

    You can mark a row and choose Display. In the following Details box, you see the information for the selected treasury position, the new book value of the treasury position in position currency and in valuation currency and all other position component values (empty fields aren’t displayed).

  • The results are stored in database tables.
